pactMagic.note: All Pact Magic slots are always cast at the highest available slot level.

classDescription: A short-rest Pact Magic caster who recovers spell slots every short rest — supplemented by Eldritch Invocations that permanently expand at-will abilities, and a patron subclass that reshapes the whole spell and feature identity.
roleIdentity: Short-rest caster with high-impact invocations and at-will tricks.

warlock-eldritch-patron-subclass.summary: Choose your Otherworldly Patron at level 1. Your patron shapes your expanded spell list and grants subclass features at levels 1, 6, 10, and 14.
warlock-eldritch-patron-subclass.description: At level 1, you forge a pact with an Otherworldly Patron — an Archfey, Fiend, Great Old One, or similar entity. Your patron choice determines your expanded spell options and grants you subclass features at levels 1, 6, 10, and 14. Each patron reshapes your spell identity and provides unique abilities that no other Warlock subclass replicates.

warlock-pact-magic.summary: Charisma-based spellcasting with a small number of spell slots that all recharge on a Short or Long Rest. Slots are always cast at your highest available pact slot level.
warlock-pact-magic.description: Your spellcasting ability is Charisma. You have a limited number of Pact Magic spell slots, but all of them recharge on a Short Rest or Long Rest. All Pact Magic slots are cast at your current maximum slot level — 1st at levels 1–2, 2nd at levels 3–4, 3rd at levels 5–6, 4th at levels 7–8, and 5th at levels 9–20. Slot count: 1 slot at level 1, 2 slots at levels 2–10, 3 slots at levels 11–16, and 4 slots at levels 17–20.
warlock-pact-magic.recovery: Pact Magic slots refresh on a Short or Long Rest.

warlock-eldritch-blast.summary: A ranged spell attack that deals 1d10 Force damage at 120-foot range. Fires additional beams as you level: 2 beams at level 5, 3 at level 11, 4 at level 17. Roll a separate attack for each beam.
warlock-eldritch-blast.description: Eldritch Blast is a cantrip (evocation). Range: 120 feet. You make one ranged spell attack against a creature. On a hit, the target takes 1d10 Force damage. The number of beams increases with your Warlock level: 2 beams at level 5, 3 beams at level 11, and 4 beams at level 17. You can direct all beams at the same target or split them among different targets. Roll a separate attack roll for each beam. Many Eldritch Invocations modify how Eldritch Blast behaves.

warlock-eldritch-invocations-2.summary: Learn 2 Eldritch Invocations at level 2. Gain one additional invocation at levels 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15, 17, and 19. You can replace one known invocation each time you gain a Warlock level.
warlock-eldritch-invocations-2.description: Eldritch Invocations are supernatural boons granted by your patron. You learn 2 invocations at level 2 and gain additional invocations as you level up (one more at levels 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15, 17, and 19). Whenever you gain a Warlock level, you can replace one invocation you know with a different one you qualify for. Some invocations have a minimum Warlock level requirement.

choices.name: Agonizing Blast
choices.description: When you cast Eldritch Blast, add your Charisma modifier to the damage of each beam that hits.

choices.name: Repelling Blast
choices.description: When Eldritch Blast hits a creature, you can push that creature up to 10 feet directly away from you.

choices.name: Eldritch Spear
choices.description: When you cast Eldritch Blast, its range is 300 feet.

choices.name: Devil's Sight
choices.description: You can see normally in darkness — both magical and nonmagical — to a distance of 120 feet.

choices.name: Mask of Many Faces
choices.description: You can cast Disguise Self at will, without expending a spell slot.

warlock-magical-cunning.summary: As a Magic action, once per Long Rest, if you have no Pact Magic slots remaining you can regain half your maximum number of Pact Magic slots (rounded up).
warlock-magical-cunning.description: Once per Long Rest, you can use a Magic action to call on your patron for aid. If you have expended all of your Pact Magic spell slots, you immediately regain a number of Pact Magic slots equal to half your maximum (rounded up). For example, a level 2 Warlock with a maximum of 2 slots regains 1 slot. This ability has no effect if you still have any Pact Magic slots remaining.

warlock-pact-boon.summary: Choose Pact of the Blade (summon a magical weapon as a Bonus Action), Pact of the Chain (cast Find Familiar with special familiar options; familiar can attack using your Reaction), or Pact of the Tome (gain a Book of Shadows with 3 cantrips from any class list).
warlock-pact-boon.description:
At level 3, your patron grants you a Pact Boon. Choose one of the following:

**Pact of the Blade:** As a Bonus Action, you can summon a pact weapon in your empty hand. You choose its form — any simple or martial melee weapon — each time you summon it. You are proficient with it while you wield it, and it counts as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity. The weapon disappears if it is more than 5 feet away from you for 1 minute or more, or if you use this feature again.

**Pact of the Chain:** You can cast Find Familiar as a ritual without expending a spell slot. When you do, you can choose the familiar's form from the standard list or from the following special forms: imp, pseudodragon, quasit, sprite, or slaad tadpole. Additionally, when you take the Attack action, you can forgo one of your own attacks to allow your familiar to make one attack of its own using your Reaction.

**Pact of the Tome:** Your patron gives you a grimoire called the Book of Shadows. When you gain this boon, choose three cantrips from any class's spell list (the spells needn't be from the same list). While the book is on your person, you can cast those cantrips at will. They count as Warlock spells for you. If you lose the Book of Shadows, you can perform a 1-hour ceremony to receive a replacement from your patron.

choices.name: Pact of the Blade
choices.description: As a Bonus Action, summon a magical pact weapon you are proficient with. It counts as magical. Unlocks invocations such as Thirsting Blade and Lifedrinker.

choices.name: Pact of the Tome
choices.description: Gain the Book of Shadows: choose 3 cantrips from any class list. You can cast them at will as Warlock spells while the book is on your person.

choices.name: Pact of the Chain
choices.description: Cast Find Familiar as a ritual without a spell slot, choosing from special forms (imp, pseudodragon, quasit, sprite, slaad tadpole). Your familiar can attack using your Reaction.

warlock-mystic-arcanum-6th.summary: Choose one 6th-level spell from the Warlock spell list. You can cast it once per Long Rest without expending a spell slot. This spell is separate from your Pact Magic slots.
warlock-mystic-arcanum-6th.description: Your patron bestows a Mystic Arcanum — a powerful spell beyond your Pact Magic. Choose one 6th-level spell from the Warlock spell list as your arcanum. You can cast this spell once without expending a spell slot. You must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it this way again. At higher levels you gain additional arcana: a 7th-level spell at Warlock level 7 (gained from the warlock-mystic-arcanum-7th feature), an 8th-level spell at level 9, and a 9th-level spell at level 11.

choices.name: Mass Suggestion Arcanum
choices.description: Cast Mass Suggestion (6th level) once per Long Rest without a spell slot: suggest a course of activity to up to 12 creatures that can hear and understand you.

choices.name: Circle of Death Arcanum
choices.description: Cast Circle of Death (6th level) once per Long Rest without a spell slot: 8d6 Necrotic damage to all creatures in a 60-foot-radius sphere (Con save halves).

choices.name: True Seeing Arcanum
choices.description: Cast True Seeing (6th level) once per Long Rest without a spell slot: a willing creature gains truesight and can see hidden doors and into the Ethereal Plane to 120 ft for 1 hour.

warlock-cantrips-known-3.summary: You now know 3 Warlock cantrips. Choose one additional cantrip from the Warlock spell list.
warlock-cantrips-known-3.description: At level 4, your cantrips known increases to 3. Choose one additional cantrip from the Warlock spell list. Cantrips can be cast at will without expending a spell slot.

warlock-pact-slot-level-3.summary: Your Pact Magic spell slots are now 3rd level. All spells cast using Pact Magic slots are automatically cast at 3rd level.
warlock-pact-slot-level-3.description: At level 5, your Pact Magic slot level increases to 3rd. Because all your Pact Magic slots are always cast at your highest available slot level, every spell you cast using a Pact Magic slot is automatically upcast to 3rd level with no additional cost. Your slot count remains 2.

warlock-contact-patron.summary: You can communicate directly with your patron. Use a Magic action to telepathically contact your patron — they can answer questions or offer guidance at the DM's discretion.
warlock-contact-patron.description: Your bond with your Otherworldly Patron has grown strong enough to allow direct communication. You can use a Magic action to telepathically contact your patron. Your patron can hear you, and you can hear a response. Your patron is not compelled to answer truthfully, and the nature of the exchange depends on your patron and the DM. This feature does not grant mechanical effects beyond the communication itself.

warlock-hex-master.summary: You can cast Hex without expending a spell slot, and it no longer requires Concentration. Once cast this way, Hex lasts until dispelled or you finish a Long Rest.
warlock-hex-master.description: At this level, your mastery of cursing magic means Hex costs you nothing to maintain. You can cast Hex once without expending a Pact Magic slot; the spell cast this way does not require Concentration and lasts until it is dispelled or until you finish a Long Rest. Once you cast Hex in this way, you must finish a Long Rest before you can do so again. You can still cast Hex using a Pact Magic slot normally.

warlock-eldritch-master.summary: As a Magic action, spend 1 minute in earnest entreaty to your patron to regain all expended Pact Magic spell slots. Usable once per Long Rest.
warlock-eldritch-master.description: At level 20, you can entreat your patron for aid in extremis. As a Magic action, you can spend 1 minute in earnest supplication and bargaining with your patron. At the end of that minute, you regain all expended Pact Magic spell slots. Once you use this feature, you must finish a Long Rest before you can use it again.

warlock-asi-or-feat.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each (neither above 20), or gain a feat you qualify for.
warlock-asi-or-feat.description: Your ability scores improve or you gain a feat. Choose one: increase one ability score of your choice by 2 (maximum 20), increase two different ability scores by 1 each (maximum 20 each), or gain a feat for which you meet the prerequisites. Warlock ASI levels occur at levels 4, 8, 12, 16, and 19.

warlock-patron-feature-6.summary: Your Otherworldly Patron grants you a subclass feature at level 6. The specific ability depends on your chosen patron.
warlock-patron-feature-6.description: At level 6, your pact with your Otherworldly Patron deepens, granting you a new feature determined by your subclass. This feature is unique to your patron: Fiend Warlocks, Archfey Warlocks, and Great Old One Warlocks each receive different abilities that reflect the nature of their bargain.

warlock-patron-feature-10.summary: Your Otherworldly Patron grants you a subclass feature at level 10. The specific ability depends on your chosen patron.
warlock-patron-feature-10.description: At level 10, your patron grants you another subclass feature. This is one of four subclass features you receive across levels 1, 6, 10, and 14. Consult your chosen patron subclass for the specific mechanical effect.

warlock-patron-feature-14.summary: Your Otherworldly Patron grants you a subclass feature at level 14. This is the final feature in your patron's progression.
warlock-patron-feature-14.description: At level 14, your patron grants you the final feature of your subclass progression. This is the most powerful of the four patron features (levels 1, 6, 10, 14) and represents the fullest expression of your pact. Consult your chosen patron subclass for the specific mechanical effect.

warlock-patron-feature-18.summary: Your Otherworldly Patron grants you a subclass feature at level 18.
warlock-patron-feature-18.description: At level 18, your patron grants you an additional subclass feature. Note that the standard Warlock subclass progression ends at level 14; if a feature appears here it is specific to certain patron subclasses that extend beyond that baseline. Consult your subclass for the specific mechanical effect.

warlock-mystic-arcanum-7th.summary: Choose one 7th-level spell from the Warlock spell list. You can cast it once per Long Rest without expending a spell slot.
warlock-mystic-arcanum-7th.description: At level 7, your patron bestows a second Mystic Arcanum. Choose one 7th-level spell from the Warlock spell list. You can cast this spell once without expending a spell slot. You must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it this way again. This arcanum is completely separate from your Pact Magic slots and does not consume them.

choices.name: Forcecage Arcanum
choices.description: Cast Forcecage (7th level) once per Long Rest without a spell slot: create a 20-foot cube cage of force bars or a solid box, trapping creatures inside with no saving throw.

choices.name: Plane Shift Arcanum
choices.description: Cast Plane Shift (7th level) once per Long Rest without a spell slot: transport yourself and up to eight willing creatures to another plane of existence, or banish an unwilling creature (Cha save).

choices.name: Finger of Death Arcanum
choices.description: Cast Finger of Death (7th level) once per Long Rest without a spell slot: deal 7d8+30 Necrotic damage to one creature (Con save halves). A humanoid killed by it rises as a zombie under your control.

warlock-mystic-arcanum-8th.summary: Choose one 8th-level spell from the Warlock spell list. You can cast it once per Long Rest without expending a spell slot.
warlock-mystic-arcanum-8th.description: At level 9, your patron bestows a third Mystic Arcanum. Choose one 8th-level spell from the Warlock spell list. You can cast this spell once without expending a spell slot. You must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it this way again. This arcanum is completely separate from your Pact Magic slots.

choices.name: Power Word Stun Arcanum
choices.description: Cast Power Word Stun (8th level) once per Long Rest without a spell slot: stun one creature with 150 hit points or fewer — it makes a Con save at the end of each of its turns to end the effect.

choices.name: Demiplane Arcanum
choices.description: Cast Demiplane (8th level) once per Long Rest without a spell slot: open a shadowy door to a 30-foot-cube extradimensional space for 1 hour.

choices.name: Feeblemind Arcanum
choices.description: Cast Feeblemind (8th level) once per Long Rest without a spell slot: deal 4d6 Psychic damage and reduce a creature's Intelligence and Charisma to 1 (Int save negates); the creature can't cast spells or use magic items.

warlock-mystic-arcanum-9th.summary: Gain a 9th-level Mystic Arcanum, your highest non-slot magical expression.
warlock-mystic-arcanum-9th.description: This is your top-end arcanum lane and should be treated separately from pact slots in both planning and sheet clarity.

choices.name: Foresight Arcanum
choices.description: Choose elite all-day reliability for yourself or a key ally.

choices.name: True Polymorph Arcanum
choices.description: Choose a reality-warping transformation tool for major scenes.

choices.name: Psychic Scream Arcanum
choices.description: Choose battlefield-wide psychic devastation and disruption.

warlock-mystic-arcanum-improvement.summary: One of your Mystic Arcanum options improves in reliability or impact.
warlock-mystic-arcanum-improvement.description: Arcanum improvements are meaningful power jumps and should change how you plan major encounters around your premium spells.

warlock-cantrips-known-4.summary: Your at-will package expands to four cantrips.
warlock-cantrips-known-4.description: Warlocks live on at-will pressure between pact-slot spikes, so this expansion should immediately feel useful in live combat loops.

warlock-pact-slot-level-5.summary: All pact slots now cast at level 5, maximizing each slot spend.
warlock-pact-slot-level-5.description: Because every pact slot casts at the same highest tier, this jump significantly boosts every slot spell you cast.

warlock-pact-slots-4.summary: You now hold four pact slots between short rests.
warlock-pact-slots-4.description: This increases your short-rest casting endurance while preserving the warlock identity of few but high-impact slots.

warlock-epic-boon.summary: Claim an epic boon to capstone your warlock progression.
warlock-epic-boon.description: Epic boons represent late-game specialization and should reinforce your patron and invocation identity rather than dilute it.

warlock-eldritch-invocation-choice-l3.summary: Choose one additional Eldritch Invocation at level 3.
warlock-eldritch-invocation-choice-l3.description: Each additional invocation should reinforce a clear warlock plan: blast pressure, pact utility, survivability, or battlefield control.

choices.name: Agonizing Blast
choices.description: Boost Eldritch Blast damage with Charisma.

choices.name: Repelling Blast
choices.description: Add push control to Eldritch Blast hits.

choices.name: Eldritch Spear
choices.description: Increase Eldritch Blast range.

choices.name: Thirsting Blade
choices.description: Blade boon synergy: pressure with additional weapon attacks.

choices.name: Lifedrinker
choices.description: High-level blade boon finisher for weapon attacks.

warlock-eldritch-invocation-choice-l5.summary: Choose one additional Eldritch Invocation at level 5.
warlock-eldritch-invocation-choice-l5.description: Each additional invocation should reinforce a clear warlock plan: blast pressure, pact utility, survivability, or battlefield control.

progressionSummary.summary: Eldritch Patron (Subclass), Pact Magic, Eldritch Blast.

buildTips.primaryReason: powers your spells and Eldritch Blast accuracy
buildTips.secondaryReason: improves durability and Concentration on control spells
buildTips.avoidDumpReason: you still need AC and initiative to stay safe at range
